expectations.text: layout.txt organizes by feature (auth/, billing/, users/) rather than primarily by horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: src/features/ contains auth/, users/, billing/, health/ vertical slices each owning their routes, service, repo, schema, dto, and tests.

expectations.text: layout.txt colocates test files next to source files rather than in a distant tests/ tree
expectations.evidence: Each feature directory contains its own .test.ts file (auth.test.ts, users.test.ts, billing.test.ts, health.test.ts) alongside sources.

expectations.text: layout.txt contains concrete domain directories like auth, billing, or users
expectations.evidence: Layout explicitly contains features/auth/, features/users/, and features/billing/.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly addresses naming — avoiding generic names or requiring domain-specific names
expectations.evidence: Section 2 covers file/type/function casing and suffixes but does not explicitly forbid generic names (e.g. utils.ts, handler.ts) nor require domain-specific verbs.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md gives a concrete file-size guideline with a number
expectations.evidence: Section 3 states 'Hard cap: 200 lines per file' and 'Functions: 40 lines max.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md requires type annotations on public / module-boundary functions
expectations.evidence: No explicit requirement for type annotations on exported/boundary functions; Zod validation at edges is covered but return-type annotations are not mandated.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md mentions tests/lint/typecheck as the verification loop
expectations.evidence: Section 11 requires PRs to 'pass bun run check (typecheck + lint + test).'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md discourages dynamic/metaprogramming patterns for agent-traceability
expectations.evidence: Section 12: 'No magic. No decorators, no DI containers, no metadata reflection. Plain functions and objects.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explains at least one decision in terms of AI-agent behavior
expectations.evidence: Section 12 (AI-agent notes) explains that stable paths and named exports make grep/refactor predictable for agents.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md recommends branded / nominal types for IDs (e.g. UserId as branded string) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 7 specifies ULID strings as primary keys but never introduces branded/nominal types like type UserId = string & { __brand }.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md prefers discriminated unions (or equivalent) over magic-string dispatch / string-keyed registries — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Discriminated unions are never mentioned and there is no guidance against magic-string dispatch or string-keyed registries.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md addresses dependency pinning, version pinning, or 'boring stack' / training-data hygiene — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 5 mandates 'Pinned versions. No ^ or ~ in package.json for production deps; exact versions' and lists a core allowlist.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects (clock, fetch, env as arguments) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 8 mentions transaction rollback and msw but not seeded randomness, frozen clocks, or injectable clock/fetch/env dependencies.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly discourages barrel / re-export index files — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Section 4: 'No barrel files except db/schema.ts ... Barrels defeat tree-shaking and hurt agent navigation.'
